mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market tightening. This reduces slack in the economy, increasing inflation expectations and pushing the 10-year Treasury yield upward as investors demand higher real returns to compensate for expected price pressures.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136877.0 thousand as of Dec 2013
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with tightening labor slack
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of 20-day windows following months with PAYEMS growth > 150k, or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 2-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero (0.09%) with inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 233.887 implies deeply negative real rates. As the Fed signals tapering of quantitative easing, real rate expectations rise, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall as investors flee duration risk and repricing occurs.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.2%
regime_hint: late-stage quantitative easing with tapering expectations
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 60% of triggered 20-day windows, or the interaction term (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1091 thousand indicate accelerating residential construction demand. This signals confidence in future economic growth, raising long-term inflation expectations and pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher as investors demand compensation for expected nominal growth.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1091.0 thousand as of Nov 2013
regime_hint: housing recovery phase with rising construction confidence
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 50% of 60-day windows following HOUST increases > 8% QoQ, or Rank IC < 0.06 over rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 101.28 shows manufacturing capacity utilization near trend. Sustained INDPRO growth above 0.3% monthly signals supply-side constraints tightening, reducing slack and increasing inflation risk, which pushes the 10-year Treasury yield upward.
regime_hint: any regime with positive supply growth
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of 20-day windows with INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.3%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 6.7% is elevated relative to pre-crisis levels. A decline in UNRATE below 6.5% signals labor market tightening that reduces economic slack, raising inflation expectations and pushing the 10-year Treasury yield upward as the Fed faces pressure to normalize policy.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines > 0.3 percentage points in 3 months
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of 60-day windows following UNRATE drops > 0.3pp in 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 431,879 million and housing starts (HOUST) at 1091 thousand jointly signal strong aggregate demand. When both series show month-over-month growth > 0.5%, the economy is overheating, inflation expectations rise sharply, and the 10-year Treasury yield rises as investors demand higher compensation.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 431879.0 million and HOUST at 1091.0 thousand as of Nov-Dec 2013
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand acceleration
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 60% of triggered 20-day windows, or the interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST) shows Rank IC < 0.06 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and GDPC1 real GDP growth at 15839.3 billion (2017 dollars), the real federal funds rate is deeply negative. If inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains above 2% while the Fed maintains zero rates, real rate expectations become increasingly negative, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall as investors seek duration protection.
evidence_refs: fact:Real rates deeply negative in early 2014
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with persistent inflation above target
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 55% of triggered 60-day windows, or the three-way interaction shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 101.28 combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 136877 thousand shows both supply and labor inputs are growing. When INDPRO growth exceeds 0.4% monthly while PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k monthly, the economy is operating near capacity, inflation pressures build, and the 10-year Treasury yield rises.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.4% AND PAYEMS monthly change > 150 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:Both series show post-recession recovery momentum
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor expansion with tightening slack
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 60% of triggered 20-day windows, or the interaction term (INDPRO × PAYEMS) shows Rank IC < 0.06 over any 18-month rolling window.
